output.summary: Fixed critical TSX/PHP sync issues where role='listitem' attributes were not rendering in frontend HTML. Changed from invalid standalone attribute syntax to correct theme_block_props() second parameter pattern in both TextCard.php and ImageCard.php.

filesModified.change: Moved role='listitem' from standalone attribute to theme_block_props() second parameter
filesModified.reason: FQA-A11Y-001: Standalone attribute syntax `<div role="listitem"` before theme_block_props() creates invalid HTML and causes role to not render. Fixed by passing [ 'role' => 'listitem' ] as second parameter to theme_block_props().

filesModified.change: Moved role='listitem' from standalone attribute to theme_block_props() second parameter
filesModified.reason: FQA-A11Y-002: Identical issue to TextCard - standalone attribute syntax creates invalid HTML. Fixed by passing [ 'role' => 'listitem' ] as second parameter to theme_block_props().

filesVerified.details: role='list' is correctly applied as standalone attribute on line 32. This is valid because the div uses plain class="" attribute, not theme_block_props(). QA confirmed this renders correctly in HTML.

issuesFixed.issue: role='listitem' not rendering in frontend HTML for TextCard
issuesFixed.rootCause: Invalid PHP syntax - role attribute placed as standalone text before theme_block_props() call, followed by separate closing `>`. This creates malformed HTML where role is stripped by WordPress.
issuesFixed.solution: Pass role as second parameter to theme_block_props(): [ 'role' => 'listitem' ]
issuesFixed.verification: Build passes. Frontend HTML will now include role='listitem' on TextCard wrapper divs.

issuesFixed.issue: role='listitem' not rendering in frontend HTML for ImageCard
issuesFixed.rootCause: Same as FQA-A11Y-001 - invalid standalone attribute syntax before theme_block_props()
issuesFixed.solution: Pass role as second parameter to theme_block_props(): [ 'role' => 'listitem' ]
issuesFixed.verification: Build passes. Frontend HTML will now include role='listitem' on ImageCard wrapper divs.

incorrect.problem: role attribute placed outside theme_block_props() call creates invalid HTML syntax. WordPress rendering strips or ignores the standalone attribute.

correct.explanation: theme_block_props() accepts two parameters: $classes (array) and $props (array). Custom HTML attributes like role must be passed in $props array as second parameter.
correct.reference: inc/helpers/blocks.php lines 90-98 show how $props are merged and passed to get_block_wrapper_attributes()

warnings: Webpack performance warnings (asset size limits) - pre-existing, not related to changes
buildStatus.details: Build completed successfully with 'webpack compiled with 3 warnings'. All warnings are pre-existing performance recommendations. No TypeScript or PHP syntax errors.

syncStatus.details: TSX files already used correct pattern (role in useBlockProps object). PHP files now match TSX pattern by passing role through theme_block_props() second parameter. Editor and frontend will produce identical ARIA structure.

beforeFix.screenReaderAnnouncement: Grid announced as 'list', but cards not identified as list items

afterFix.screenReaderAnnouncement: Grid announced as 'list of N items', each card announced as 'list item'

qaVerificationRequired.check: Verify role='listitem' renders in frontend HTML
qaVerificationRequired.note: This should now pass with correct count

qaVerificationRequired.expectedBehavior: Grid announced as 'list of N items', each card announced as 'list item' when navigating
qaVerificationRequired.note: QA was previously blocked on this due to role='listitem' not rendering

qaVerificationRequired.expectedBehavior: nth-child CSS selectors still work, masonry stagger pattern intact
qaVerificationRequired.note: QA confirmed role='list' didn't break layout, role='listitem' should also be safe

themeBlockPropsFunction.firstParameter: Array of class names with boolean conditions (conditional class builder pattern)
themeBlockPropsFunction.secondParameter: Array of additional HTML attributes (role, data-*, aria-*, etc.)
themeBlockPropsFunction.implementation: Lines 90-98 merge $props into $args and pass to get_block_wrapper_attributes()
themeBlockPropsFunction.usage: Always pass custom HTML attributes through $props, never as standalone text before the function call

whyStandaloneAttributesFail.explanation: When role="listitem" is placed before the theme_block_props() call, the HTML structure becomes: `<div role="listitem" [theme_block_props output] >`. This creates invalid syntax where the role attribute is orphaned before the rest of the attributes are added. WordPress's rendering engine may strip or ignore the malformed attribute.
whyStandaloneAttributesFail.correctPattern: theme_block_props() outputs all attributes including id, class, and aria-labelledby. Custom attributes must be merged into this output by passing them in the $props parameter, not added as text before the function call.

testPage.note: Test page unchanged - fixes are PHP template corrections only
